qa_pairs.question: How do I run Intel VTune Profiler in graphical mode on VDI nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: To run VTune Profiler in graphical mode on VDI nodes, first connect to gra-vdi.alliancecan.ca using TigerVNC. Then, load the required modules by executing `module load CcEnv StdEnv/2020 vtune`, and finally launch the graphical interface with `vtune-gui`.

qa_pairs.question: What modules need to be loaded to prepare for profiling an MPI program with Intel VTune Profiler?
qa_pairs.answer: To prepare for profiling an MPI program with Intel VTune Profiler, you need to load the standard environment and the VTune module. This is done by running `module load StdEnv/2020` followed by `module load vtune`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I execute an MPI program to collect profiling data using Intel VTune Profiler within a job or interactive session?
qa_pairs.answer: After compiling your MPI program, you can run it to collect profiling data within a job or an interactive session (started by a `salloc` command) using the command `srun aps your_mpi_program.x`.
